summary: Analyzed a hypnotic techno, raw techno project with 14 tracks, 109 clips, 0 automated tracks, at 132 BPM — 224 bars total. Found 5 potential improvements. Generated 7 completion actions (0 critical, 4 high priority).

actions.title: Add Bass / Sub Element
actions.description: No bass track detected. Add a sub bass (30–80 Hz) that locks to the kick pattern or runs a continuous groove. Single-note bass with filter automation creates movement.
actions.rationale: Sub bass provides low-end weight and energy on large sound systems.

actions.title: Add Filter Sweep Automation
actions.description: Automate filter cutoff on key tracks through section transitions (bars 17–65). Build from fully closed to 60–70% open over 4–8 bars leading into the drop, close again entering breakdown.
actions.rationale: Filter sweeps are the primary energy tool in techno and are expected by listeners.

actions.title: Add Tension Sweeps at Section Boundaries
actions.description: Add white noise or filtered sweep risers (4–8 bars) before major section changes. Section boundaries at: bar 17 before Build, bar 65 before Intro, bar 97 before Breakdown, bar 129 before Peak. Also add downward pitch automation on synths as the drop hits.
actions.rationale: Sweeps signal section changes and build anticipation — critical for floor impact.

actions.title: Add Sidechain: KICK → CLICK
actions.description: No sidechain relationship detected. AI proposes adding a kick-triggered sidechain compressor on 'CLICK' sourced from 'KICK'. Apply through the groove section (bars 129–161). Purpose: Texture pumping — sidechain creates rhythmic movement in pads/synths. Use Ableton's Compressor with Sidechain > Audio From set to the kick track.
actions.rationale: Texture pumping — sidechain creates rhythmic movement in pads/synths

mutationPayloads.reason: Add Compressor with sidechain input from KICK

actions.title: Add Send Level Automation
actions.description: Automate reverb/delay send amounts on key elements (bars 97–129). Throw reverb sends up during breakdown to create space and anticipation.
actions.rationale: Automated send throws create depth and atmosphere essential for techno tension.

actions.title: Add Drum Fill at Key Transitions
actions.description: Add a 1–2 bar drum fill (bars 15–17) before each major section change. A 1/16th–1/32nd note roll on snare or clap works well.
actions.rationale: Fills signal transitions and energize the moment of section change.

actions.title: Add Resonance Automation
actions.description: Automate filter resonance on 'CLICK' through the peak section (bars 129–161). Rising resonance (Q) from 0.5 to 2.0 creates tension and urgency.
actions.rationale: Resonance automation adds harmonic tension absent from purely gain-based dynamics.

weaknesses: Very little automation detected — arrangement feels static and lacks dynamic movement
Section 'Peak': weak or absent transition into this section
No bass/sub track detected — low-end continuity may be weak
2 return track(s) detected but may be underused (no automated sends)
11 tracks with many clips but zero automation — consider adding filter sweeps, send throws, or gain automation
rationale: This hypnotic techno project has been analyzed using rule-based music analysis. Completion suggestions are based on: arrangement structure analysis, role inference (track type detection without relying on names), section energy/density profiling, automation density analysis (0 tracks with automation detected), sidechain graph analysis (1 links detected or proposed), and genre-specific production conventions for hypnotic techno. All suggestions include exact bar placements derived from the parsed arrangement, machine-executable mutation payloads, and confidence scores based on parse quality (100%) and detection certainty.
